content.full_text:
State of California 
Memorandum 
To 
File 
Date 
Sept. 29, 1983 
No. 1.\-1-83-221 
From 
: 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
Janis Shank McLean ~ 
Subject: 
Awards of Contracts Under Gov. Code Section 84308 
The Light Rail Commission will award a construction contract 
for $25 million. 
One of the bidders gave a $500 contribution to 
a member. 
How does 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18438.2's statement 
that awards of contracts aren't "applications for licenses, 
permits, or other entitlements for use" apply? 
If the award of the contract is: 
1. 
Under competitive bidding, or 
2. 
Totaly discretionary, then the action is 
quasi-legislative and comes within the meaning of 2 Cal. 
Adm. Code Section 18438(b) • 
If the person involved has a legal right regarding the 
contract (for example, if he or she was denied a contract under 
competitive bidding) the decision concerning this is 
quasi-judicial. 
JSM:plh

sections.question: One of the bidders gave a $500 contribution to a member. How does 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18438.2's statement that awards of contracts aren't "applications for licenses, permits, or other entitlements for use" apply?
sections.conclusion: If the award of the contract is: 1. Under competitive bidding, or 2. Totally discretionary, then the action is quasi-legislative and comes within the meaning of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18438(b). If the person involved has a legal right regarding the contract (for example, if he or she was denied a contract under competitive bidding) the decision concerning this is quasi-judicial.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a contract award by the Light Rail Commission for a $25 million construction project, where one bidder made a $500 contribution to a member, qualifies as an application for licenses, permits, or other entitlements under 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18438.2.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Contract awards are quasi-legislative in nature and fall within Section 18438(b) whether made through competitive bidding or total discretion. However, if a person has a legal right regarding the contract (such as being denied a contract under competitive bidding), the decision becomes quasi-judicial.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a memorandum to file from FPPC staff member Janis Shank McLean. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers present; question and conclusion content extracted from body text. Parsing succeeded despite lack of standard section formatting.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: One of the bidders gave a $500 contribution to a member. How does 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18438.2's statement that awards of contracts aren't "applications for licenses, permits, or other entitlements for use" apply?

CONCLUSION: If the award of the contract is: 1. Under competitive bidding, or 2. Totally discretionary, then the action is quasi-legislative and comes within the meaning of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18438(b). If the person involved has a legal right regarding the contract (for example, if he or she was denied a contract under competitive bidding) the decision concerning this is quasi-judicial.

Code Section 84308 The Light Rail Commission will award a construction contract for $25 million. One of the bidders gave a $500 contribution to a member. How does 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18438.2's statement that awards of contracts aren't "applications for licenses, permits, or other entitlements for use" apply? If the award of the contract is: 1. Under competitive bidding, or 2. Totaly discretionary, then the action is quasi-legislative and comes within the meaning of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18438(b) • If the person involved has a legal right regarding the contract (for example, if he or she was denied a contract under competitive bidding) the decision concerning this is quasi-judicial. embedding.summary: The FPPC advises on the application of Government Code Section 84308 regarding contract awards by the Light Rail Commission, clarifying that such awards constitute quasi-legislative actions under competitive bidding or discretionary circumstances, but become quasi-judicial if a party has a legal right regarding the contract.
